evals.prompt: Create a ragbits chat application called assistant — a general-purpose chat that streams responses from gpt-4o-mini. No extra features.
evals.expected_output: An assistant/ directory with pyproject.toml (ragbits-chat only), README.md, assistant/__init__.py, and assistant/app.py. app.py subclasses ChatInterface, implements async chat() that calls LiteLLM.generate_streaming and yields self.create_text_response per chunk. Main block launches RagbitsAPI(Chat).run(). Dependencies installed.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: assistant/ directory exists

assertions.name: app_file_exists
assertions.description: assistant/app.py exists and imports ChatInterface, ChatContext, ChatResponse, LiteLLM

assertions.name: init_file_exists
assertions.description: assistant/__init__.py exists so the directory is a valid package

assertions.name: pyproject_has_only_chat
assertions.description: pyproject.toml depends on ragbits-chat and does NOT list ragbits-agents or ragbits-document-search (no extra features were requested)

assertions.name: chat_method_streams
assertions.description: chat() calls llm.generate_streaming and yields self.create_text_response(chunk) in an async for loop

assertions.name: main_block_uses_ragbitsapi
assertions.description: The __main__ block imports RagbitsAPI and calls RagbitsAPI(Chat).run() without an auth_backend argument

assertions.name: readme_lists_three_run_commands
assertions.description: README.md lists all three run commands: ragbits api run ..., python -m ..., and python path/to/app.py

assertions.name: conversation_history_enabled
assertions.description: Chat class has conversation_history = True (or explicitly sets it, so prior turns are replayed)

evals.prompt: Build a ragbits chat application called weather-bot — an agent that calls a weather API tool, streams its reasoning as live updates, and has a branded UI with a weather-themed title and starter questions.
evals.expected_output: A weather-bot/ directory. pyproject.toml depends on ragbits-chat AND ragbits-agents. app.py has a tools.py (or an inline TOOLS list) with a weather-fetching tool, instantiates self.agent = Agent(llm, tools=TOOLS), and its chat() method uses agent.run_streaming and pattern-matches ToolCall/ToolCallResult events into self.create_live_update(..., LiveUpdateType.START/FINISH). UICustomization has a weather-themed title, subtitle, logo, and 2+ starter questions drawn from the weather domain. Dependencies installed.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: weather-bot/ directory exists

assertions.name: agents_dependency_declared
assertions.description: pyproject.toml lists ragbits-agents in addition to ragbits-chat

assertions.name: agent_instantiated_in_init
assertions.description: app.py creates self.agent = Agent(llm=..., tools=...) inside __init__

assertions.name: tool_is_domain_specific
assertions.description: At least one tool function relates to weather, forecast, temperature, or location lookup (not just example_tool unchanged)

assertions.name: chat_uses_run_streaming
assertions.description: chat() calls self.agent.run_streaming(...) and pattern-matches on ToolCall/ToolCallResult events

assertions.name: tool_events_become_live_updates
assertions.description: ToolCall yields a LiveUpdateType.START live update; ToolCallResult yields LiveUpdateType.FINISH with the same id

assertions.name: ui_customization_weather_themed
assertions.description: ui_customization has a weather-themed title, a weather-appropriate logo/emoji, and 2+ starter questions tied to weather queries

assertions.name: text_chunks_still_stream
assertions.description: chat() still yields self.create_text_response(chunk) for str events so the final reply streams into the UI

evals.prompt: Create a ragbits chat app called hr-assistant that answers employee HR questions using the company's policy documents. It needs a web UI, login-protected access, like/dislike feedback forms, and document search.
evals.expected_output: An hr-assistant/ directory. pyproject.toml depends on ragbits-chat, ragbits-document-search, and ragbits-core. app.py defines LikeForm/DislikeForm Pydantic models, a get_auth_backend() factory with ListAuthenticationBackend + InMemorySessionStore + at least one seed user, a Chat class with conversation_history=True, feedback_config referencing both forms, a cached _get_document_search() using InMemoryVectorStore + LiteLLMEmbedder + auto-ingest from a documents/ folder, and a chat() method that yields references before streaming the grounded answer. The __main__ block calls RagbitsAPI(Chat, auth_backend=get_auth_backend()).run(). README documents the seed credentials and ingestion workflow.

assertions.name: project_directory_created
assertions.description: hr-assistant/ directory exists

assertions.name: document_search_deps_declared
assertions.description: pyproject.toml lists ragbits-document-search AND ragbits-core in addition to ragbits-chat

assertions.name: documents_folder_exists
assertions.description: hr-assistant/documents/ exists with at least one sample file so the app can ingest something on first run

assertions.name: auth_backend_factory_present
assertions.description: app.py defines get_auth_backend() returning a ListAuthenticationBackend with InMemorySessionStore and at least one seed user

assertions.name: feedback_forms_defined
assertions.description: app.py defines LikeForm and DislikeForm with json_schema_serialization_defaults_required=True and required fields

assertions.name: feedback_config_attached
assertions.description: Chat class has a feedback_config class attribute referencing LikeForm and DislikeForm

assertions.name: document_search_cached
assertions.description: app.py has a _get_document_search (or get_document_search) helper that caches the DocumentSearch instance so in-memory data isn't lost between turns

assertions.name: auto_ingest_on_first_call
assertions.description: The cached factory iterates documents/ and calls document_search.ingest(...) the first time it is invoked

assertions.name: chat_yields_references_before_answer
assertions.description: chat() calls self.create_reference(...) for retrieved elements and THEN streams the LLM answer via self.create_text_response

assertions.name: main_block_passes_auth_backend
assertions.description: __main__ calls RagbitsAPI(Chat, auth_backend=get_auth_backend()).run() — not the default single-arg form

assertions.name: readme_documents_credentials_and_ingest
assertions.description: README lists the seed credentials (flagged as demo-only) and tells the user how to add documents
